hero.name: Taranjit Kang
hero.tagline: From building my own computers to architecting enterprise systems — a full-stack engineer with a passion for innovation. I drive digital transformation with Java, Spring Boot, React, and the cloud at companies like Intuit and Royal Bank of Canada.

about.title: A developer, by passion and by trade
about.p1: Taranjit Kang (Tarn) — a Canadian citizen born in Ontario, now residing in the USA on a green card. I've been a full-stack developer my entire career, fuelled by a deep passion for technology that began in childhood.
about.p2: Growing up I was a computer nerd. I got my first IBM PC at age 8, and later started building my own machines for gaming and experimenting with code long before any formal education.
about.p3: My professional journey spans Java, Spring Boot, React, and a wide range of front-end and back-end technologies — working for notable fintech companies like Intuit and Royal Bank of Canada.
about.p4: I'm committed to continuous learning and growth in an ever-evolving tech landscape.
about.location: Born in Canada · Based in the USA

skills.title: Technologies I build with
skills.subtitle: A decade of hands-on work across the full stack — from backend microservices to polished front-end experiences and the cloud infrastructure underneath.

experience.title: Where I've made an impact
experience.subtitle: A brief history of the teams and products I've helped ship, from the most recent to the earliest. The technologies span backend to frontend: Java, Spring Boot, React, TypeScript, Python, AWS, Docker, Kubernetes, Jenkins, and more.

items.adobe: As a Senior Backend Engineer on the Catalog Engineering team, I build reliable, scalable services on the Adobe Unified Platform that let business teams launch and manage commerce products. I ship production-ready Java, Spring Boot, and AWS systems following microservice best practices, with Ruby and React/Next.js across the stack, and observability via Splunk, New Relic, Grafana, and Kibana.
items.handshake: As a Senior Engineer, I shipped A/B-tested consumer features behind LaunchDarkly toggles that drove over $20M in annual recurring revenue — roughly 10% of company ARR. Built with Ruby on Rails, React, and TypeScript, integrating Stripe for payments and Google Cloud / BigQuery for data-driven decisions.
items.intuit: Developed and enhanced features for TurboTax and QuickBooks, popular tax-preparation software. Full-stack development in Java and React for the homepage and pre/post-auth experiences. Contributed to the TurboTax homepage now used by millions of users.
items.rbc: Worked on the RBC Mobile Banking app, developing Java microservices for the Virtual Visa Debit feature and contributing to enhancements across pages such as Transactions.
items.ncr: Enhanced the NCR Teller Application for US Bank, integrating Java Spring Boot microservices with a YAML-based SDK and deploying via Docker and Kubernetes. Led CI/CD with Jenkins, wrote Cucumber E2E tests, and managed releases with Bitbucket and Jira.
items.rogers: Worked on Rogers-owned The Shopping Channel e-commerce website — a monolithic project with C# and an Angular front end. Contributed to key components including Product Search, Product Details, and Product Listing pages.

projects.title: Things I've been building
projects.subtitle: Live products I've shipped plus open-source projects from my GitHub — side projects, tools, and things I build to keep learning.

testimonials.title: What colleagues say
testimonials.subtitle: Recommendations and feedback from coworkers across the companies and roles throughout my career.

contact.title: Let's build something great
contact.subtitle: Open to senior and staff full-stack roles, consulting, and interesting collaborations. The fastest way to reach me is email or LinkedIn.
